CP00-571 is a descriptor-only RP18 P06 workflow-permission slice (40 units: M04 tail 12 + M05 head 28) that holds every boundary required for closeout: no real data, no state writes, no runtime dispatch, Claude strictly read-only and not final approver, and Hermes used only for review packet/receipt descriptors. The pack honestly declares `production_ready:false`/`runtime_ready:false`/`implemented_pending_review` rather than over-claiming, all pre-review validation commands pass, and contract↔code parity is enforced by a strict `deepEqual`. CP178+ review-hardening evidence (auth preflight, no forbidden tools, schema present, summary-only prohibited) is clean, and unrelated dirty files are correctly scoped out. The pack does NOT over-claim production readiness: manifest declares production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=implemented_pending_review; command-evidence declares production_ready=false, status=implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout. The production_ready_candidate=true flag is strictly coverage.valid && slice.valid, not an enterprise-trust claim.

Scope integrity verified across review-baseline, live closeout-pack-plan.json (CP00-571 is the first live planned pack), registry CP571_PACK_BINDING/REQUIREMENTS, contract cp571_requirements, and manifest: range RP18.P06.M04.S19-RP18.P06.M05.S28, unit_count 40, micro-phase counts 12/28, deliverable distribution {test:8, hermes_evidence:2, claude_review:3, implementation:16, security_audit:7, ui:4}=40, no duplicate unit IDs, RP18-only.

No-write/no-real-data boundary confirmed: grep for writeFile/fetch/child_process/exec/spawn/process.env/fs in packages/ai-legal-workflows/src returned no matches; freezeCp571Result stamps ~30 false runtime/write flags plus no_real_data:true and synthetic_only:true; per-row guards enforce descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false; contract no_write_attestation confirms writes_product_state/creates_database_rows/updates_database_rows/reads_object_storage/writes_object_storage/emits_hermes_runtime_receipt/executes_command_runtime/loads_real_fixture_data all false; 7 no-leak guards present.

Authority boundary intact: review-request read_only:true with tools Read,Grep,Glob; descriptor authority_boundary claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true; no_write_attestation promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false; tests assert all of these. Hermes control-plane pattern is used only for deterministic review packet/receipt descriptors (descriptor_only:true, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false) — not product runtime.

Validation evidence: all pre-review commands exit 0 (node --check x5, node --test, rp05-rp18 validates, closeout-pack-plan:validate, weighted/spec/rp00/goal/fullplan validate, npm test, npm run validate, npm run build, git diff --check). Contract<->code sync is enforced by a strict deepEqual in the validator script (contract.p06_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor === generated cp571Descriptor; cp571_requirements and cp571_no_write_attestation deepEqual the registry). Post-review gated commands (closeout-pack:validate, review run/normalize/receipt) are appropriately deferred per hardened_review_sequence.

CP178+ review hardening clean: readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, stdout 1228 bytes, valid JSON result — not 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped); validation-report shows forbidden_tools_present:false, review_schema_present:true, summary_only_review_allowed:false (correctly prohibited for Risk B + security/audit + review-evidence deliverables), source_inspection_mode read_tools_used, prompt 2217 bytes < 25600, no full diff/source embedded, no active untracked implementation files omitted; invalid_attempts_preserved:[] for this pack. Unrelated dirty files (scripts/progress-control-room-data.mjs, tools/progress-control-room/index.html, docs/ldip-integration LDIP invalid attempt, workbook/* docs) are correctly classified as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved and excluded from pack scope.
